rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign clauses.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and component ports in AGREE expressions only when they are explicitly declared in the same scope.
Define constants and equations in component type annexes when they describe interface behavior.
Encode boolean logic using standard AGREE syntax with named expressions for clarity.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid component ports or variables in their scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type scopes.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Deadline or Period as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints not tied to visible model features
